content: We can reframe state-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems as being anchored by an internal curiosity-driven autonomous meta-agent that prioritizes which unlabeled new data to encode into long-term memory rather than encoding all new experience indiscriminately. This meta-agent leverages established ICM and RND curiosity mechanisms to rank memory storage candidates by how much encoding them will reduce the overall system’s future uncertainty, aligning active memory updating directly with the core goals of active learning.
